items.content_statement: The legal status of marriage and civil partnership, including that they carry legal rights, benefits and protections that are not available to couples who are cohabiting or who have, for example, undergone a non-legally binding religious ceremony.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about legal facts and distinctions - factual content about how legal systems work that learners must hold

items.content_statement: Cohabitants do not obtain marriage-like status or rights from living together or by having children
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - factual claim about legal status that learners must hold

items.content_statement: How families and relationships change over time, including through birth, death, separation and new relationships
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about family lifecycle patterns and relationship transitions - factual/conceptual content about how the world works

items.content_statement: characteristics of successful parenting
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about parenting characteristics - factual content to be learned

items.content_statement: the importance of the early years of a child's life for brain development
items.classification_rationale: conceptual understanding of causal relationship between early years and brain development

items.content_statement: Judge when a relationship is unsafe and where to seek help when needed, including when pupils are concerned about violence, harm, or when they are unsure who to trust
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for it - requires reasoning across multiple factors (violence indicators, harm signs, trust assessment) with no single correct answer but better and worse judgments

items.content_statement: The role of consent, trust, mutual respect, honesty, kindness, loyalty, shared interests and outlooks, generosity, boundaries, tolerance, privacy, and the management of conflict, reconciliation and ending relationships in romantic relationships
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about how relationship elements function - conceptual understanding that can be assessed through explanation of roles and connections

items.content_statement: treat others with kindness and respect, including in public spaces and including strangers
items.classification_rationale: sustained default orientation - treating others with kindness and respect operates as an ongoing enacted capacity across contexts, not deployed only when situations call for it
prerequisite_lts: understand the legal rights and responsibilities regarding equality, and that everyone is unique and equal

items.content_statement: understand the legal rights and responsibilities regarding equality, and that everyone is unique and equal
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge - conceptual understanding of legal frameworks and equality principles that must be grasped

items.content_statement: The importance of self-esteem, independence and having a positive relationship with oneself, and how these characteristics support healthy relationships with others
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about causal relationships - how self-esteem and independence affect relationship quality

items.content_statement: Developing one's own interests, hobbies, friendship groups, and skills
items.classification_rationale: Sustained orientation - ongoing development of personal interests and skills across contexts over time

items.content_statement: What it means to be treated with respect by others
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - conceptual understanding of respectful treatment

items.content_statement: skills for communicating respectfully within relationships and with strangers, including in situations of conflict
items.classification_rationale: occasion-triggered analytical capability deployed when communication situations arise; multiple valid approaches exist; reasoning quality determines effectiveness

items.content_statement: The different types of bullying (including online bullying)
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about categories and definitions - hard prerequisite sequence where understanding types is foundational

items.content_statement: The impact of bullying
items.classification_rationale: Conceptual understanding of causal relationships and consequences - builds on knowing what bullying is
prerequisite_lts: The different types of bullying (including online bullying)

items.content_statement: How and where to get help regarding bullying
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when a bullying situation calls for it - reasoning about appropriate help-seeking strategies

items.content_statement: Responsibilities of bystanders to report bullying
items.classification_rationale: Sustained orientation that operates by default across contexts - enacted responsibility as a bystander
prerequisite_lts: The different types of bullying (including online bullying)

items.content_statement: Skills for ending relationships or friendships with kindness and managing the difficult feelings that endings might bring, including disappointment, hurt or frustration
items.classification_rationale: Describes analytical/reasoning capability deployed when a relationship-ending situation calls for it - requires judgment about how to apply kindness and emotional management strategies to specific contexts

items.content_statement: understand that ethical behaviour goes beyond consent and involves kindness, care, attention to the needs and vulnerabilities of the other person, as well as an awareness of power dynamics
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about the components and nature of ethical behaviour - a conceptual understanding that can be assessed through explanation

items.content_statement: understand that just because someone says yes to doing something, that doesn't automatically make it ethically ok
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about the relationship between consent and ethical acceptability - a conceptual principle that can be assessed through explanation

items.content_statement: How stereotypes, in particular stereotypes based on sex, gender reassignment, race, religion, sexual orientation or disability, can cause damage (e.g. how they might normalise non-consensual behaviour or encourage prejudice)
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about causal relationships - how stereotypes function and their harmful effects

items.content_statement: Recognise misogyny and other forms of prejudice
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for identifying prejudice - occasion-triggered skill requiring reasoning across contexts
prerequisite_lts: How stereotypes, in particular stereotypes based on sex, gender reassignment, race, religion, sexual orientation or disability, can cause damage

items.content_statement: How inequalities of power can impact behaviour within relationships, including sexual relationships. For example, how people who are disempowered can feel they are not entitled to be treated with respect by others or how those who enjoy an unequal amount of power might, with or without realising it, impose their preferences on others.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about how power dynamics function in relationships - a conceptual understanding of causal mechanisms that can be taught and assessed through explanation

items.content_statement: How pornography can negatively influence sexual attitudes and behaviours by normalising harmful sexual behaviours and by disempowering some people, especially women, to feel a sense of autonomy over their own body and providing some people with a sense of sexual entitlement to the bodies of others
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about causal relationships - how pornography affects attitudes and behaviours through specific mechanisms

items.content_statement: Discuss how some sub-cultures might influence our understanding of sexual ethics, including the sexual norms endorsed by so-called "involuntary celibates" (incels) or online influencers
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when examining cultural influences on sexual ethics - multiple valid interpretive frameworks exist for understanding sub-cultural influence

items.content_statement: The same expectations of behaviour apply in all contexts, including online
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - a conceptual principle about consistency of behavioural expectations across contexts that learners must understand

items.content_statement: the importance of being cautious about sharing personal information online and of using privacy and location settings appropriately to protect information online
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about online safety principles and protective measures

items.content_statement: the difference between public and private online spaces and related safety issues
items.classification_rationale: conceptual understanding of online space distinctions and their safety implications

items.content_statement: Social media accounts may be fake and may post things which aren't real or have been created with AI
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about how social media works - factual content that must be understood

items.content_statement: Social media users may say things in more extreme ways than they might in face-to-face situations
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about social media behavior patterns - factual content that must be understood

items.content_statement: Some social media users present highly exaggerated or idealised profiles of themselves online
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about social media presentation patterns - factual content that must be understood

items.content_statement: Any material provided online might be circulated, and that once this has happened there is no way of controlling where it ends up
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about how digital sharing works - a factual claim about the nature of online material distribution

items.content_statement: The serious risks of sending material to others, including the law concerning the sharing of images
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about legal and personal risks - factual content about consequences and legal frameworks

items.content_statement: Not to provide material to others that they would not want to be distributed further and not to pass on personal material which is sent to them
items.classification_rationale: Sustained orientation - a default practice of respecting privacy and exercising restraint in sharing personal material across contexts
prerequisite_lts: Any material provided online might be circulated, and that once this has happened there is no way of controlling where it ends up
The serious risks of sending material to others, including the law concerning the sharing of images

items.content_statement: That keeping or forwarding indecent or sexual images of someone under 18 is a crime, even if the photo is of themselves or of someone who has consented, and even if the image was created by the child and/or using AI generated imagery
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about legal facts - hard prerequisite sequence applies

items.content_statement: The potentially serious consequences of acquiring or generating indecent or sexual images of someone under 18, including the potential for criminal charges and severe penalties including imprisonment
items.classification_rationale: Conceptual understanding of consequences - builds on legal knowledge prerequisite
prerequisite_lts: That keeping or forwarding indecent or sexual images of someone under 18 is a crime

items.content_statement: How to seek support and that they will not be in trouble for asking for help, either at school or with the police, if an image of themselves has been shared
items.classification_rationale: Factual knowledge about support procedures

items.content_statement: That sharing indecent images of people over 18 without consent is a crime
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about legal facts

items.content_statement: What to do and how to report when they are concerned about material that has been circulated, including personal information, images or videos, and how to manage issues online
items.classification_rationale: Describes analytical capability deployed when situations call for it - responding to concerning online material requires situation-specific judgment and reasoning across multiple considerations

items.content_statement: the prevalence of deepfakes including videos and photos
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about factual information regarding deepfake prevalence

items.content_statement: how deepfakes can be used maliciously as well as for entertainment
items.classification_rationale: conceptual understanding of deepfake applications and purposes

items.content_statement: the harms that can be caused by deepfakes
items.classification_rationale: conceptual understanding of deepfake consequences and impacts

items.content_statement: how to identify deepfakes
items.classification_rationale: analytical skill deployed when encountering potential deepfake content

items.content_statement: The internet contains inappropriate and upsetting content, some of which is illegal, including unacceptable content that encourages misogyny, violence or use of weapons
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about the nature of internet content - factual claim about how the world works

items.content_statement: Where to go for advice and support about something they have seen online
items.classification_rationale: Factual knowledge about specific resources and procedures - hard prerequisite sequence applies

items.content_statement: That online content can present a distorted picture of the world and normalise or glamorise behaviours which are unhealthy and wrong
items.classification_rationale: Propositional understanding about how online content functions - conceptual knowledge to be internalised

items.content_statement: How to identify when technology and social media is used as part of bullying, harassment, stalking, coercive and controlling behaviour, and other forms of abusive and/or illegal behaviour and how to seek support about concerns.
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for it - recognising patterns of harmful behaviour across digital contexts and determining appropriate support responses

items.content_statement: That pornography, and other online content, often presents a distorted picture of people and their sexual behaviours and can negatively affect how people behave towards sexual partners. This can affect pupils who see pornographic content accidentally as well as those who see it deliberately. Pornography can also portray misogynistic behaviours and attitudes which can negatively influence those who see it.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about causal relationships - how pornography affects behaviour and attitudes. This is factual/conceptual content about how the world works that learners must understand.

items.content_statement: That websites may share personal data about their users, and information collected on their internet use, for commercial purposes (e.g. to enable targeted advertising).
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about how websites operate commercially - factual content about data sharing practices that learners must understand

items.content_statement: That criminals can operate online scams, for example using fake websites or emails to extort money or valuable personal information. This information can be used to the detriment of the person or wider society.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about how online scams operate and their potential harms - factual content to be understood

items.content_statement: About risks of sextortion
items.classification_rationale: Factual knowledge about specific risks - propositional content to be understood

items.content_statement: How to identify online scams relating to sex
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when encountering potential scams - occasion-triggered skill requiring reasoning and evaluation

items.content_statement: How to seek support if they have been scammed or involved in sextortion
items.classification_rationale: Capability deployed when a specific situation arises - occasion-triggered skill for responding to particular circumstances

items.content_statement: AI chatbots are an example of how AI is rapidly developing, and that these can pose risks by creating fake intimacy or offering harmful advice
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about AI chatbot risks - factual content that must be understood

items.content_statement: critically think about new types of technology as they appear online and how they might pose a risk
items.classification_rationale: analytical capability deployed when encountering new technology - occasion-triggered reasoning skill

items.content_statement: How to recognise, respect and communicate consent and boundaries in relationships, including in early romantic relationships (in all contexts, including online) and early sexual relationships that might involve kissing or touching
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when relationship situations call for it - recognising and communicating about consent requires reasoning across multiple contexts

items.content_statement: That kindness and care for others requires more than just consent
items.classification_rationale: Propositional claim about how relationships work - a conceptual understanding about the relationship between consent and care

items.content_statement: identifying, resisting and understanding pressure in relationships from peers or others, including sexual pressure
items.classification_rationale: analytical capability deployed when situations call for it - recognising and responding to pressure requires reasoning across multiple contexts

items.content_statement: how to avoid putting pressure on others in relationships
items.classification_rationale: analytical capability deployed when situations call for it - avoiding putting pressure on others requires reasoning about impact and appropriate behaviour

items.content_statement: how to determine whether other children, adults or sources of information are trustworthy, how to judge when a relationship is unsafe (and recognise this in the relationships of others)
items.classification_rationale: analytical capability deployed when situations call for it - evaluating trustworthiness and safety requires reasoning across multiple contextual factors

items.content_statement: how to seek help or advice, including reporting concerns about others, if needed
items.classification_rationale: analytical capability deployed when situations call for it - determining when and how to seek help requires contextual judgment

items.content_statement: Ways of seeking help when needed and how to report harmful behaviour
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - specific procedures and methods that can be learned and recalled

items.content_statement: That there are strategies they can use to increase their safety, and that this does not mean they will be blamed if they are victims of harmful behaviour
items.classification_rationale: Conceptual understanding - causal relationship between safety strategies and victim-blaming that must be understood

items.content_statement: That in some situations a person might appear trustworthy but have harmful intentions
items.classification_rationale: Conceptual understanding - propositional claim about how appearances can be deceptive

items.content_statement: How to increase their personal safety in public spaces, including when socialising with friends, family, the wider community or strangers
items.classification_rationale: Occasion-triggered analytical skill - deployed when specific safety situations arise, requires reasoning about context

items.content_statement: Trusting their instincts when something doesn't feel right
items.classification_rationale: Sustained default orientation - ongoing attunement to internal warning signals across contexts

items.content_statement: Sexual harassment and sexual violence are unacceptable, and it is never the fault of the person experiencing it
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - factual claims about the nature of sexual harassment/violence and responsibility attribution that learners must understand

items.content_statement: Sexual harassment includes unsolicited sexual language/attention/touching, taking and/or sharing intimate or sexual images without consent, public sexual harassment, pressuring other people to do sexual things, and upskirting
items.classification_rationale: Definitional/propositional content - factual knowledge about what constitutes sexual harassment

items.content_statement: sexual harassment and sexual violence among young people but also includes other forms of concerning behaviour like using age-inappropriate sexual language
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about what constitutes harmful sexual behaviour - factual content that must be understood

items.content_statement: The concepts and laws relating to domestic abuse, including controlling or coercive behaviour, emotional, sexual, economic or physical abuse, and violent or threatening behaviour
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about legal concepts and definitions - factual content that must be understood

items.content_statement: The concepts and laws relating to harms which are exploitative, including sexual exploitation, criminal exploitation and abuse, grooming, and financial exploitation.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about legal concepts and definitions that must be understood - hard prerequisite sequence applies as legal understanding builds on foundational concepts

items.content_statement: The Domestic Abuse Act 2021 recognised children who see, hear, or experience the effects of abuse, and are related to either the victim of the abusive behaviour, or the perpetrator, as victims of domestic abuse in their own right (part 1 section 3)
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about legal definitions and statutory recognition - factual content about what the Act establishes

items.content_statement: The Domestic Abuse Act 2021 statutory guidance is designed to support statutory and non-statutory bodies working with victims of domestic abuse, including children
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about the purpose and scope of statutory guidance - factual content about what the guidance is designed to do

items.content_statement: It is illegal to assist in the performance of FGM, virginity testing or hymenoplasty, in the UK or abroad, or to fail to protect a person under 16 for whom they are responsible
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about legal requirements - factual content about what constitutes illegal behaviour under UK law

items.content_statement: Strangulation (applying pressure to the neck) is an offence, regardless of whether it causes injury
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - factual claim about legal status that learner must hold

items.content_statement: That any activity that involves applying force or pressure to someone's neck or covering someone's mouth and nose is dangerous and can lead to serious injury or death.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about causal relationships - factual claim about physical dangers that learners must understand

items.content_statement: That pornography presents some activities as normal which many people do not and will never engage in, some of which can be emotionally and/or physically harmful.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about pornography's representation of sexual activities - factual content about media representation that learners must understand

items.content_statement: How to seek support for their own worrying or abusive behaviour or for worrying or abusive behaviour they have experienced from others, including information on where to report abuse, and where to seek medical attention when required, for example after an assault.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about procedures and resources - factual information that can be learned and recalled

items.content_statement: Sex, for people who feel ready and are over the age of consent, can and should be enjoyable and positive
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - a factual/value claim about the nature of consensual sexual experience that learners should understand

items.content_statement: that many young people wait until they are older, and that people of all ages can enjoy intimate and romantic relationships without sex
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about relationship patterns and possibilities - factual content to be understood

items.content_statement: Sexual consent can be given, withheld or removed at any time, even if initially given
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about how consent works - factual content about legal and ethical principles

items.content_statement: Considerations that people might take into account prior to sexual activity include the law, faith and family values
items.classification_rationale: Factual knowledge about factors that influence sexual decision-making

items.content_statement: Kindness and care for others require more than just consent
items.classification_rationale: Conceptual understanding about the relationship between consent and broader ethical considerations in relationships

items.content_statement: All aspects of health can be affected by choices they make in sex and relationships, positively or negatively, e.g. physical, emotional, mental, sexual and reproductive health and wellbeing
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about causal relationships - how choices affect health outcomes across multiple domains

items.content_statement: The facts about the full range of contraceptive choices, efficacy and options available, including male and female condoms
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about contraceptive facts, efficacy, and options - factual content that must be understood

items.content_statement: Signposting towards medically accurate online information about sexual and reproductive health to support contraceptive decision-making
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for contraceptive decision-making support - occasion-triggered skill
prerequisite_lts: The facts about the full range of contraceptive choices, efficacy and options available, including male and female condoms

items.content_statement: medically and legally accurate and impartial information on all pregnancy options, including keeping the baby, adoption, abortion and where to get further help
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about pregnancy options - factual content that must be understood

items.content_statement: How risk can be reduced through safer sex including through condom use and the use and availability of HIV prevention drugs Pre-Exposure Prophylaxis (PrEP) and Post-Exposure Prophylaxis
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about specific risk reduction methods and prevention drugs - factual content that must be understood

items.content_statement: Exposure Prophylaxis (PEP) and how and where to access them
items.classification_rationale: Factual knowledge about what PEP is and access procedures - propositional content

items.content_statement: The importance of, and facts about, regular testing
items.classification_rationale: Conceptual understanding of why regular testing matters and factual knowledge about testing

items.content_statement: The role of stigma
items.classification_rationale: Conceptual understanding of how stigma functions in relation to STI testing and treatment

items.content_statement: The prevalence of STIs, the short and long term impact they can have on those who contract them and key facts about treatment.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - factual content about STI prevalence, impacts, and treatment facts that learners must hold

items.content_statement: How and where to seek support for concerns around sexual relationships including sexual violence or harms
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about support resources and procedures - factual content that can be learned and assessed through knowledge check

items.content_statement: Where to access confidential sexual and reproductive health advice and treatment
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about specific resources and access points - factual content that can be directly assessed

items.content_statement: How to talk about their emotions accurately and sensitively, using appropriate vocabulary
items.classification_rationale: Describes analytical capability deployed when situations call for emotional communication - requires reasoning about context, audience, and appropriate expression

items.content_statement: participation and volunteering or acts of kindness for mental wellbeing and happiness
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about causal relationships - how specific activities affect mental wellbeing outcomes

items.content_statement: understand what makes them feel happy and what makes them feel unhappy
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about personal emotional triggers - factual self-knowledge that can be understood and explained

items.content_statement: recognising that loneliness can be for most people an inevitable part of life at times and is not something of which to be ashamed
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about the nature of loneliness as a normal human experience - conceptual understanding that can be explained

items.content_statement: That worrying and feeling down are normal, can affect everyone at different times and are not in themselves a sign of a mental health condition, and that managing those feelings can be helped by seeing them as normal.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about the nature of emotions and mental health - factual claims about what is normal vs. pathological that learners must understand

items.content_statement: Factual information about the prevalence and characteristics of common types of mental ill health and more serious mental health conditions
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - factual content about prevalence and characteristics that learners must hold

items.content_statement: The distinction between normal feelings and mental health conditions, understanding that normal feelings should not be labelled as mental health conditions
items.classification_rationale: Conceptual understanding - transferable principle about appropriate categorisation and labelling

items.content_statement: It's possible to overcome barriers to participating in fun, enjoyable or rewarding activities using coping strategies, and that finding the courage to participate in activities which initially feel challenging may decrease anxiety over time rather than increasing it
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about how anxiety and participation work - causal claims about what happens when people use coping strategies and engage with challenging activities

items.content_statement: That gambling can lead to serious mental health harms, including anxiety, depression, and suicide, and that some gambling products are more likely to cause these harms than others
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about causal relationships between gambling and mental health outcomes - factual content to be understood

items.content_statement: That the co-occurrence of alcohol/drug use and poor mental health is common and that the relationship is bi-directional: mental health problems can increase the risk of alcohol/drug use, and alcohol/drug use can trigger mental health problems or exacerbate existing ones.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about causal relationships - factual/conceptual claims about how mental health and substance use interact

items.content_statement: That stopping smoking can improve people's mental health and decrease anxiety.
items.classification_rationale: Factual knowledge about the effects of smoking cessation on mental health

items.content_statement: the benefits of limiting time spent online, the risks of excessive time spent on electronic devices and the impact of positive and negative content online on their own and others' mental and physical wellbeing
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about causal relationships - how online time and content affect wellbeing

items.content_statement: the impact of unhealthy or obsessive comparison with others online including through setting unrealistic expectations for body image
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about causal relationships - how online comparison affects wellbeing

items.content_statement: how people may curate a specific image of their life online
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about how online presentation works

items.content_statement: the impact that an over-reliance on online relationships, including relationships formed through social media, can have
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about causal relationships - how over-reliance on online relationships affects wellbeing

items.content_statement: how to report, or find support, if they have been affected by harmful behaviours online
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when a situation calls for it - reasoning about appropriate reporting channels and support options based on specific circumstances

items.content_statement: The risks related to online gambling and gambling-like content within gaming, including the accumulation of debt
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about specific risks and consequences - factual content about what risks exist and their effects

items.content_statement: understanding the prevalence of misinformation and disinformation online, including conspiracy theories
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about how misinformation/disinformation operates online - factual content to be understood

items.content_statement: how to be a discerning consumer of information online
items.classification_rationale: analytical capability deployed when encountering information online - reasoning skill across multiple evaluative frameworks
prerequisite_lts: understanding the prevalence of misinformation and disinformation online, including conspiracy theories

items.content_statement: how advertising and information is targeted at them
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about targeting mechanisms - factual content about how systems work

items.content_statement: The risks of illegal behaviours online, including drug and knife supply or the sale or purchasing of illicit drugs online
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about specific risks and illegal activities - factual content that learners must hold

items.content_statement: The serious risks of viewing online content that promotes self-harm, suicide or violence
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about risks - factual content that must be understood

items.content_statement: How to safely report material that promotes self-harm, suicide or violence
items.classification_rationale: Occasion-triggered procedural skill deployed when encountering harmful content

items.content_statement: How to access support after viewing content that promotes self-harm, suicide or violence
items.classification_rationale: Occasion-triggered procedural skill deployed when support is needed after exposure

items.content_statement: the links between a poor diet and health risks, including tooth decay, unhealthy weight gain, and cardiovascular disease
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about causal relationships between diet and specific health outcomes - factual content to be understood

items.content_statement: The risks of unhealthy weight gain include increased risks of cancer, type 2 diabetes and cardiovascular disease
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about causal relationships between weight gain and health outcomes - factual content to be understood

items.content_statement: The facts about which drugs are illegal, the risks of taking illegal drugs, including the increased risk of potent synthetic drugs being added to illegal drugs, the risks of illicit vapes containing drugs, illicit drugs and counterfeit medicines, and the potential health harms, including the link to poor mental health
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - factual content about drug classifications, risks, and health consequences that learners must hold

items.content_statement: What constitutes low risk alcohol consumption in adulthood, and the legal age of sale for alcohol in England
items.classification_rationale: Factual/propositional knowledge - specific definitions and legal facts that can be learned and recalled

items.content_statement: Understanding how to increase personal safety while drinking alcohol, including how to decrease the risks of having a drink spiked or of poisoning from potentially fatal substances such as methanol
items.classification_rationale: Conceptual understanding of safety principles and risk reduction strategies - hierarchical knowledge that builds on understanding of alcohol effects and safety concepts

items.content_statement: alcohol dependency
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about a specific condition that learners must understand as factual content

items.content_statement: The facts about the multiple serious harms from smoking tobacco (particularly the link to lung cancer and cardiovascular disease), the benefits of quitting and how to access support to do so
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - factual content about health effects, benefits, and support resources that learners must hold

items.content_statement: The facts about vaping, including the harms posed to young people, and the role that vapes can play in helping adult smokers to quit
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about factual claims regarding vaping effects and uses - hard prerequisite sequence applies as these are specific facts to be learned

items.content_statement: treatment and prevention of infection, and about antibiotics
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about medical facts and procedures that must be understood - hard prerequisite sequence exists for understanding infection treatment before prevention strategies

items.content_statement: Dental health and the benefits of good oral hygiene, including brushing teeth twice a day with fluoride toothpaste and cleaning between teeth, reducing consumption of sugar-containing food and drinks, and regular check-ups at the dentist
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about dental health practices and their benefits - factual content to be understood

items.content_statement: the benefits of regular self-examination and screening
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about health benefits - factual content to be understood

items.content_statement: The facts and scientific evidence relating to vaccination, immunisation and antimicrobial resistance
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - factual and scientific content that learners must hold

items.content_statement: screen-free time before bed and removing phones from the bedroom are important for good-quality sleep
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about sleep hygiene practices - factual content to be understood

items.content_statement: how a lack of sleep can affect weight, mood and ability to learn
items.classification_rationale: causal relationship knowledge - conceptual understanding of sleep's effects on multiple health outcomes

items.content_statement: The importance of pre-conception health, including taking folic acid
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about health facts and recommendations - Type 1 factual content

items.content_statement: The importance of pelvic floor health
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about health facts - Type 1 factual content

items.content_statement: Information on miscarriage and pregnancy loss, and how to access care and support
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about health information and support systems - Type 1 factual content

items.content_statement: what a GP is; when to use A&E / minor injuries; accessing sexual health and family planning clinics; the role of local pharmacies; and how to seek help via local third sector partners which may have specialist services
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about healthcare system components and their functions - factual content that can be learned and assessed through knowledge checks

items.content_statement: Before age 16, a child's parents will have responsibility for consenting to medical treatment on their behalf unless they are Gillick competent to take this decision for themselves
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about legal framework - factual content about how medical consent works for minors

items.content_statement: Pupils should understand the circumstances in which someone over 16 may not be deemed to have capacity to make decisions about medical treatment.
items.classification_rationale: This is propositional knowledge about legal/medical concepts that learners must understand - specific factual content about capacity and medical consent that can be assessed through explanation or knowledge checks.

items.content_statement: identify risk and manage personal safety in increasingly independent situations, including around roads, railways – including level crossings - and water (including the water safety code), and in unfamiliar social or work settings (for example the first time a young person goes on holiday without their parents)
items.classification_rationale: analytical or reasoning capability deployed when situations call for it - risk assessment and safety management across multiple contexts requires situational analysis and judgment

items.content_statement: How to recognise and manage peer influence in relation to risk-taking behaviour and personal safety, including peer influence online and on social media
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations involving peer influence arise - requires reasoning across multiple contexts and scenarios

items.content_statement: Skills to support self-awareness, self-management, social awareness, relationship skills and responsible decision making in contexts involving conflict and violence
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capabilities deployed when situations call for them - these are occasion-triggered skills for navigating social situations

items.content_statement: Skills to recognise and manage peer pressure
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when peer pressure situations arise - occasion-triggered skill

items.content_statement: Carrying weapons is uncommon among young people
items.classification_rationale: Propositional/factual content about statistical reality that learners must hold as knowledge

items.content_statement: Misconceptions about weapon prevalence can lead to the false belief that carrying a knife is necessary for protection
items.classification_rationale: Conceptual understanding of causal relationship between perception and behavior that learners must internalize

items.content_statement: seek help where there is a concern about grooming or exploitation
items.classification_rationale: occasion-triggered analytical skill - deployed when a concerning situation is recognised; involves reasoning about appropriate help-seeking responses

items.content_statement: period problems such as premenstrual syndrome; heavy menstrual bleeding; endometriosis; and polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS)
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about medical conditions - factual content that must be understood

items.content_statement: when to seek help from healthcare professionals
items.classification_rationale: conceptual understanding of decision criteria - transferable principle about healthcare seeking behaviour
prerequisite_lts: period problems such as premenstrual syndrome; heavy menstrual bleeding; endometriosis; and polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS)

items.content_statement: the facts about reproductive health, including fertility and menopause, and the potential impact of lifestyle on fertility for men and women
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about biological facts and causal relationships that can be taught and assessed through knowledge checks

items.content_statement: Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation is usually best taught after 12 years old
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - factual claim about appropriate age for CPR instruction
